content: A defined value before modifiers are applied. In most cases, it is also the printed value.

• Cards in the 'Basic' classification are cards not associated with a specific identity or aspect.
  • A player may customize the remainder of their deck using basic cards. Basic cards are designated by 'Basic' printed at the bottom of the card. Basic cards are not aspect cards.

A basic power is a statistic that allows a character to perform a certain game function. Five basic powers: Attack (ATK) — basic attack, deal damage to another character (heroes, allies, villains, minions).

Thwart (THW) — remove threat from a scheme (heroes, allies). Defense (DEF) — prevent damage from an attack (heroes only).

Recovery (REC) — heal damage from self (alter-egos only). Scheme (SCH) — place threat on the main scheme (villains, minions).

• Each time the villain attacks or schemes, it is given one facedown card from the encounter deck as a boost card; minions with the villainous keyword also get one when they activate.
  • During the activation, each boost card is turned faceup one at a time, adding boost icons to the enemy's ATK (attacking) or SCH (scheming).
  • If the boost field has a star icon, resolve the 'Boost' ability beneath the divider line when turned faceup.
  • A star icon does not itself contribute to ATK/SCH.
  • Damage from a boost ability is not considered damage from the activation.
  • After applying a boost card, discard it.
  • If dealt a boost card outside its own activation, it stays facedown until that enemy activates.
